reasoning: Milorad Dodik has a long history of threatening independence referendums without following through, using the threat as political leverage. While early 2025 has seen significant political escalations—including the March 2025 passage of a new 'secessionist' Draft Constitution by the National Assembly—the current discourse as of late April 2025 centers on how to adopt this constitution (whether by a 2/3 parliamentary majority or by a referendum). Even if a referendum is chosen to adopt the constitution, it would likely be framed as a constitutional referendum rather than explicitly a 'referendum on the independence of Republika Srpska'. Given that we are exactly two months away from the July 1, 2025 deadline and no date has been scheduled, the base rate of these threats actually materializing is extremely low. The probability of an official scheduling of an explicit independence referendum before the deadline is thus estimated at 2%.
